system2.candidate: FORTRON® PPS POLYPHENYLENE SULFIDE + 1 wt% Polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) Slip Agent

blocking_constraints.description: Polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) is not biodegradable and has an environmental degradation half‑life well beyond the 5‑year limit required for this application, violating the environmental persistence constraint. This renders the formulation non‑compliant with sustainability requirements.
blocking_constraints.suggested_mitigation: Replace PDMS with a biodegradable slip agent such as a fatty‑acid amide, sorbitan ester, or other non‑PFAS, non‑halogenated lubricant that meets thermal stability and dispersion criteria.

blocking_constraints.description: The formulation requires a laboratory twin‑screw extruder with high‑shear mixing zones and multiple heating zones to achieve the specified melt temperature, shear rate, and residence time. Existing lab setups may lack this equipment.
blocking_constraints.suggested_mitigation: Acquire or access a twin‑screw extruder meeting the design specifications outlined in Q2 (long feed/metering zones, ≥4 heating zones up to 260–270 °C, L/D≥24).

system3.feedback_to_system2: Future candidate materials must include additives that are proven biodegradable or have an environmental half‑life ≤5 years. Verify persistence compliance early in the feasibility assessment and provide concrete evidence of biodegradability to avoid hard infeasibility.

blocking_constraints.description: No documented commercial source, purity specifications, or licensing information for oleamide at the required scale and quality.
Insufficient data on the thermal stability of 1 wt% oleamide in PA11; decomposition onset relative to processing temperatures up to 290 °C is unknown.
No evidence that the PA11/oleamide blend meets FDA biocompatibility requirements or has a biodegradation half‑life ≤5 years.
Uncertain whether existing laboratory twin‑screw extruders can maintain uniform temperatures up to 290 °C and provide sufficient residence time for stable mixing.
Multiple key questions lack sufficient evidence (oleamide availability, thermal stability, dispersion behavior, biocompatibility).

system3.feedback_to_system2: Prior to evaluation, confirm commercial availability and licensing of all additives, provide thermal stability data above processing temperatures, demonstrate high‑shear dispersion, and verify biocompatibility and biodegradability compliance.
